name: Pine Nut Almond Tart
recipeInstructions:
In a food processor or with a pastry blender, combine the flour, butter and sugar. Blend until the flour mixture resembles cornmeal, then add egg and combine well. Knead the dough lightly with the heel of the hand against a smooth surface for a few seconds to distribute the butter, and reform it into a ball. Cover with plastic and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Roll out pastry and fit into a 10-inch pan with a removable bottom. Prick the bottom and place in freezer for 10 minutes. Bake in preheated 425°F oven for 10 minutes; cool.
In a small saucepan, heat apricot preserves, strain and spread over pastry. With a mixer, combine until smooth the almond paste, butter, sugar and almond extract. Add one egg at a time, mixing until well combined. Add flour and baking powder and beat until smooth. Pour into pastry and sprinkle with pine nuts. Bake in 425°F oven for 10 to 15 minutes, reduce heat to 375°F and continue to bake for 10-15 minutes until golden brown.
